published_prior_art.cite: TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch: arbitrary observational/experimental source-distribution do-calculus search; BareinboimPearl2014MZTransport: complete multi-environment transportability with limited experiments
TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch is closest but gives search-based do-calculus identification, not a k-source observational overlap-hedge witness preserving all selected source laws
ShpitserPearl2008CompleteID: ordinary ID reduction; CorreaTianBareinboim2018Adjustment: generalized adjustment under selection

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest selection-bias completeness comparator CorreaTianBareinboim2019 is missing; it proves completeness for causal-effect identification with selection bias and external data and must be separated from the proposed k-source overlap-hedge witness.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The adjustment reduction states only Z subset O_j, but the displayed source formula also requires X, Y, and Z to be observed in source j and requires explicit C-clauses justifying both P_j(Y|X,Z,S_j=1) and P_j(Z|S_j=1).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Correa, Tian, and Bareinboim 2019 as a named prior-art comparison and state precisely why its selection-bias completeness theorem does not already yield the multisource overlap-hedge certificate.
Tighten Theorem 2 by adding X union Y union Z subset O_j, the exact source-positivity denominators, and the C-clauses needed for the adjustment formula.

Add a small nondegenerate three-source binary boundary example showing identical selected pairwise source laws but different P_x(Y); this would make the overlap-hedge object concrete and protect the flagship claim from looking like a relabelled do-search failure.

recommended_next_step: Revise v2 to patch the adjustment statement and add the missing selection-bias completeness comparison before re-review.
